name: Nepal
headline: Nepal ROTATION-BACKFILL CONFIRMATION at 48.4 / Functional. Post-Gen-Z-uprising democratic-renewal arc: March 2026 first general elections held following September 2025 protests; Balendra Shah (35) sworn in as youngest PM; Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP) achieved largest majority in 60+ years. Death toll of September 2025 uprising: 76 killed (22 protesters, 3 police, 10 prisoners). Climate vulnerability (flooding/landslides) persists. New government has explicit anti-corruption mandate but no enacted legislation track record yet. Sub-threshold positive (institutional-renewal pre-translation); hold first-baseline.

AWR.rationale: Sustained. Gen-Z-uprising response demonstrated significant institutional responsiveness.

ACT.rationale: Sustained. March 2026 elections held on schedule despite institutional disruption.

EQU.rationale: Sustained. Climate-vulnerability inequality (Himalayan small-state exposure) persistent structural.

ACC.rationale: Sustained. New government anti-corruption mandate is positive signal pre-translation; the 76 deaths during September 2025 uprising require accountability tracking but already absorbed into pre-renewal baseline.

SYS.rationale: Sustained. Democratic institutional renewal (peaceful transition + new-party-majority + youngest PM) demonstrates systems resilience.

INT.rationale: Sustained. I3 institutional self-correction: democratic-renewal response to corruption protests is genuine institutional self-correction signal but not yet legislatively translated.

evidence.finding: March 27 2026: Balendra Shah (35) sworn in as Nepal's youngest PM after Gen-Z protests toppled previous government; first general election held in March 2026.
Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP) achieved largest majority in 60+ years.
September 2025 protests: at least 76 killed (22 protesters, 3 police, 10 prisoners). Parliament, hotels, palaces, PM residence set ablaze.
Gen-Z movement analysis: long-term corruption response; democratic-renewal arc.

keyEvidence: March 2026 elections held post-Gen-Z uprising; youngest PM (35) sworn in.
RSP achieved largest majority in 60+ years.
Democratic-renewal arc is genuine but pre-translation.
rationale: Rotation-backfill first-baseline confirmation at 48.4. The democratic-renewal arc (peaceful transition + new-party-majority + Gen-Z institutional response) is a genuine positive signal but per translation-from-promise threshold: hold dimensional weight until specific anti-corruption legislation enacted. Conservative-direction protocol for first-baseline. Math: (3+3+3+2.75+3+2.75+3+3)/8 = 23.5/8 = 2.9375 → ((2.9375-1)/4)*100 = 48.44 → 48.4.
whyHeadline: Nepal first-baseline 48.4; post-Gen-Z democratic renewal sustained; pre-translation hold.

dominantDimension.note: INT I3 institutional self-correction is the dominant signal (democratic renewal response to corruption); pre-translation hold.

nextForwardSignal.label: First major anti-corruption legislation passage; monsoon-season flood-response evaluation

boundaryWatchResolution: Not boundary-sensitive. 48.4 sits 7.4pt above Developing/Functional floor.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
